title: Cordillera platycalyx
taxonomy: Cordillera platycalyx is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the Rubiaceae family, which includes over 600 genera of flowering plants. The species is part of the tribe Spermacoceae, a group of plants known for their diverse range of species. Cordillera platycalyx is native to the Andes mountain range, where it grows in humid and subtropical regions. Its classification into the Rubiaceae family suggests a close relationship with other flowering plants in this group.
uses: Cordillera platycalyx has been studied for its potential medicinal uses, but more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. benefits: Cordillera platycalyx is reported to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
